title: KubeSkills Daily
description: Play with KubeSkills Daily and learn Kubernetes

items.title: Day 1 - Pod Restart Policies
items.description: Test how Kubernetes handles failures and restarts

items.title: Day 2 - StatefulSet Pod Ordering
items.description: Break ordered updates and compare OrderedReady vs Parallel management

items.title: Day 3 - OOMKilled vs Resource Limits
items.description: Trigger OOM events and compare Kubernetes QoS classes

items.title: Day 4 - Network Policy Lockouts
items.description: Enforce default deny, poke DNS/web holes, and recover access

items.title: Day 5 - Taints and Tolerations
items.description: Evict pods with NoExecute, compare tolerations, and explore NoSchedule

items.title: Day 6 - Liveness Probe Death Loops
items.description: Trigger, inspect, and fix misconfigured probes plus readiness/startup

items.title: Day 7 - Init Container Failures
items.description: Debug stuck init containers, add retries, and run sidecars

items.title: Day 8 - PVC Binding Failures
items.description: Storage that never arrives until you fix StorageClasses and modes

items.title: Day 9 - ConfigMap Updates - The Sync Gap
items.description: See why env vars freeze, volumes lag, and subPath never refreshes

items.title: Day 10 - Secret Rotation - Hot Swapping Credentials
items.description: Rotate secrets, see env/volume drift, and force rollouts to reload

items.title: Day 11 - Service DNS Propagation Delays
items.description: Watch DNS timing gaps, endpoint readiness, and headless vs ClusterIP behavior

items.title: Day 12 - HPA Thrashing - The Scale Storm
items.description: See aggressive HPAs thrash, tune behaviors, and test multi-metric scenarios

items.title: Day 13 - PodDisruptionBudgets - Blocking Maintenance
items.description: See how strict PDBs block drains, fix budgets, and explore conflict scenarios

items.title: Day 14 - ResourceQuota - Deployment Denied
items.description: See how quotas silently block pods, use LimitRanges, and explore scoped/count quotas

items.title: Day 15 - Ingress Path Routing - Order Matters
items.description: See how Prefix ordering, Exact, regex, and rewrites change nginx-ingress routing

items.title: Day 16 - RBAC Lockout - Permission Denied
items.description: See how Roles, ClusterRoles, bindings, and aggregation grant or deny access

items.title: Day 17 - Admission Webhooks - The Deployment Blocker
items.description: See how webhook failures stall the API server and how to bypass, scope, and debug them

items.title: Day 18 - CronJob Concurrency - The Job Pile-Up
items.description: See how CronJob concurrency policies affect overlapping runs, skips, replacements, and history

items.title: Day 19 - ServiceAccount Tokens - Projection Problems
items.description: See how automount settings, projected tokens, and token requests change Kubernetes auth

items.title: Day 20 - Node NotReady - The Eviction Cascade
items.description: See how NotReady taints trigger evictions, tolerations delay them, and workloads recover

items.title: Day 21 - Finalizers - The Deletion Blocker
items.description: See how finalizers block deletion, how to clear them safely, and how built-ins protect resources

items.title: Day 22 - Image Pull Secrets - Authentication Failures
items.description: See how private registry pulls fail, fix secrets, attach via ServiceAccounts, and debug pull errors

items.title: Day 23 - CNI Plugin Failures - Network Isolation
items.description: Inspect CNI configs, test pod connectivity, DNS, NetworkPolicies, MTU, CIDR exhaustion, and CNI errors

items.title: Day 24 - kube-proxy Modes - Service Routing Breakdown
items.description: Inspect kube-proxy mode, test ClusterIP/NodePort routing, iptables rules, scaling, session affinity, and failures

items.title: Day 25 - etcd Performance - The Cluster Heartbeat
items.description: Check etcd health, size, keys, load impact, compaction/defrag, backups, alarms, and metrics

items.title: Day 26 - Kubelet Eviction - Resource Pressure
items.description: Inspect node capacity and thresholds, see QoS/priority eviction order, trigger pressure, and verify recovery

items.title: Day 27 - API Server Rate Limiting - Request Throttling
items.description: Inspect APF configs, flood the API, watch throttling, review FlowSchemas/PriorityLevels, and test identities/metrics

items.title: Day 28 - Pod Security Admission - Policy Enforcement
items.description: Test baseline vs restricted PSA, fix security contexts, observe warn/audit, and validate workloads

items.title: Day 29 - Custom Resource Definitions - Validation Failures
items.description: Iterate from permissive CRDs to strict validation, defaults, status subresources, and multiple versions

items.title: Day 30 - Operator Reconciliation - Infinite Loops
items.description: See how naive controller logic loops forever and how status subresources, observedGeneration, owner refs, and backoff stop the storm

items.title: Day 31 - Helm Charts - Template Rendering Failures
items.description: Debug Helm templates: syntax errors, nil pointers, required values, value precedence, functions, lint, dry-run, upgrades, and rollbacks

items.title: Day 32 - ArgoCD Sync - Out of Sync Nightmares
items.description: Install ArgoCD, sync a repo, watch drift, auto-sync/self-heal, prune, hooks, waves, health checks, replace, selective sync, and rollback

items.title: Day 33 - Service Mesh - Traffic Split Gone Wrong
items.description: Install Istio, split traffic between versions, debug weights, missing subsets, headers, retries/timeouts, and analyze routing issues

items.title: Day 34 - Multi-Tenancy - Namespace Isolation Breakdown
items.description: Create tenant namespaces, quotas, RBAC, apps, network isolation, PSA, LimitRanges, and enforce cross-namespace boundaries

items.title: Day 35 - Init Container Dependencies - Circular Deadlocks
items.description: Build circular init waits, debug deadlocks, fix by breaking the cycle, add timeouts, retries, readiness vs init, Jobs, and StatefulSets

items.title: Day 36 - Jobs and CronJobs - Backoff Explosion
items.description: Trigger failing Jobs to see backoff, cap retries, set deadlines, tune completions and parallelism, suspend/resume, and manage CronJobs

items.title: Day 37 - Volume Mounts - Permission Denied Chaos
items.description: Explore volume permissions: emptyDir vs hostPath, fsGroup fixes, read-only mounts, subPath quirks, PVC access, shared volumes, Secrets, projected volumes, and securityContext overrides

items.title: Day 38 - DNS Resolution - CoreDNS Breakdown
items.description: Break and restore CoreDNS, inspect pod DNS config, tune ndots/timeouts, compare dnsPolicy, tweak CoreDNS, test caching, block/allow DNS, and resolve headless services

items.title: Day 39 - Resource Contention - QoS Eviction Wars
items.description: Launch pods across QoS classes, trigger memory pressure, observe evictions, apply priorities, quotas, LimitRanges, throttling, and finalizers

items.title: Day 40 - Certificates - Expiration Catastrophe
items.description: Inspect control plane certs, install cert-manager, issue short-lived and CA certs, simulate expirations and failed renewals, and debug TLS secrets

items.title: Day 41 - Prometheus Metrics - Missing Targets
items.description: Install Prometheus Operator, create Prometheus selectors, break and fix ServiceMonitors via labels/ports/policies/relabeling, add PodMonitor, and debug missing targets

items.title: Day 42 - Logging Pipelines - Lost in Transit
items.description: Ship logs with Fluent Bit, trigger parser errors, buffer pressure, destination failures, multiline issues, and sidecar patterns to see how logs vanish or get enriched

items.title: Day 43 - Distributed Tracing - Missing Spans
items.description: Install Jaeger, send traces with and without propagation, explore sampling, missing instrumentation, collector pressure, baggage, and slow spans to find trace gaps

items.title: Day 44 - Resource Metrics - Debugging Slow Pods
items.description: Verify metrics-server, stress CPU/memory/I-O, observe throttling and OOM, compare QoS, query raw metrics, drive contention, and autoscale from metrics

items.title: Day 45 - kubectl debug - Ephemeral Container Debugging
items.description: Attach ephemeral debug containers, copy pods, debug nodes, and troubleshoot pods without shells or in CrashLoop using kubectl debug

items.title: Day 46 - Probe Tuning - The Goldilocks Problem
items.description: Tune startup, liveness, and readiness probes; prevent premature restarts, manage readiness routing, and understand probe overhead and rolling update protections

items.title: Day 47 - StatefulSets - Orphaned Volume Nightmare
items.description: See how StatefulSets retain PVCs, handle scale down/up, use retention policies, expand PVCs, manage updates, and clean up orphaned storage

items.title: Day 48 - Cluster Upgrades - Version Skew Chaos
items.description: Inspect versions, deprecated APIs, CRD compatibility, skew policies, webhook versions, storage versions, and draft a safe upgrade plan

items.title: Day 49 - Backup & Restore - Velero Disasters
items.description: Install Velero with MinIO, back up and delete a namespace, restore it, note emptyDir/PVC gaps without snapshots, handle conflicts, hooks, schedules, exclusions, and storage checks

items.title: Day 50 - Node Failures - Eviction Storm Chaos
items.description: Cordon, drain, taint, and recover nodes; observe evictions, PDB blocks, StatefulSet stuck pods, DaemonSet tolerance, and custom tolerations to tune failover

items.title: Day 51 - API Server Overload - Rate Limit Hell
items.description: Hammer the API server, observe APF queues, build custom FlowSchemas/PriorityLevels, simulate webhook slowness, and plan safe rate/priority configs

items.title: Day 52 - etcd Corruption - Quorum Loss Disaster
items.description: Check etcd health/members/size, snapshot and restore, compact/defrag, monitor alarms/metrics, stress etcd, and document disaster recovery

items.title: Day 53 - Network Partitions - Split-Brain Nightmare
items.description: Model network partitions with NetworkPolicies, observe split-brain symptoms, test quorum-based apps, and plan detection and mitigation

items.title: Day 54 - Certificate Rotation - Auto-Renewal Gone Wrong
items.description: Check Kubernetes and cert-manager certs, create short-lived certs, break renewals, monitor expirations, verify chains, and document rotation/DR plans

items.title: Day 55 - RBAC Misconfigurations - Permission Denied Hell
items.description: Probe missing verbs, namespace scope, resourceNames, subresources, aggregation, impersonation, and least-privilege RBAC patterns

items.title: Day 56 - Resource Exhaustion - Cluster at Capacity
items.description: Explore pending pods, fragmentation, preemption, quotas, LimitRanges, and capacity planning under resource pressure

items.title: Day 57 - Pod Security Standards - Enforcement Failures
items.description: Compare PSA profiles and modes, common violations, and compliant workload patterns

items.title: Day 58 - Ingress Failures - Traffic Not Routing
items.description: Install ingress-nginx and debug class, backend, TLS, path, and annotation misconfigurations

items.title: Day 59 - Webhook Timeouts - Admission Controller Chaos
items.description: Explore webhook timeouts, failure policies, selectors, and recovery patterns

items.title: Day 60 - Custom Controllers - Reconciliation Loop Hell
items.description: Explore broken reconciliation loops, conflicts, retries, and recovery patterns

items.title: Day 61 - Helm Chart Failures - Deployment Gone Wrong
items.description: Debug Helm template errors, hooks, dependencies, upgrades, and rollbacks

items.title: Day 62 - CNI Plugin Failures - Networking Goes Dark
items.description: Check CNI configs, pod networking, IP exhaustion, NetworkPolicies, and diagnostics

items.title: Day 63 - CoreDNS Failures - When DNS Dies
items.description: Break and restore CoreDNS, test DNS resolution, caching, and diagnostics

items.title: Day 64 - PV Provisioning Failures - Storage Chaos
items.description: Test PVC provisioning failures, binding issues, and storage diagnostics

items.title: Day 65 - Image Pull Failures - Registry Nightmares
items.description: Trigger image pull failures, auth errors, and backoff behavior

items.title: Day 66 - Resource Quotas - Hitting the Limits
items.description: Explore quota enforcement, LimitRanges, and quota diagnostics

items.title: Day 67 - Init Containers - Startup Sequence Failures
items.description: Explore init container sequencing, failures, and diagnostics

items.title: Day 68 - Job & CronJob Failures - Batch Processing Gone Wrong
items.description: Explore Job and CronJob failures, retries, and scheduling issues

items.title: Day 69 - Service Mesh Failures - Istio/Linkerd Gone Wrong
items.description: Explore service mesh failures, mTLS, sidecars, and routing issues

items.title: Day 70 - Autoscaling Failures - HPA & VPA Gone Wrong
items.description: Explore autoscaling pitfalls with HPA/VPA, metrics, and conflicts

items.title: Day 71 - Operator Failures - Custom Controllers Breaking
items.description: Explore custom resource failures, operator RBAC issues, and CRD management problems

items.title: Day 72 - Cluster Upgrades - Breaking Changes
items.description: Explore deprecated APIs, version skew issues, and safe upgrade procedures

items.title: Day 73 - Multi-Tenancy Failures - Namespace Isolation Breaking
items.description: Explore namespace isolation failures, RBAC misconfigurations, and tenant security issues

items.title: Day 74 - Backup Disasters - When Restores Fail
items.description: Explore backup failures, incomplete restores, and disaster recovery pitfalls

items.title: Day 75 - Observability Failures - When Metrics Disappear
items.description: Explore metrics collection failures, log aggregation issues, and observability blind spots

items.title: Day 76 - GitOps Failures - When Deployments Don't Sync
items.description: Explore GitOps sync failures, manifest validation issues, drift detection, and troubleshooting deployment pipelines

items.title: Day 77 - Secret Management - Credentials Everywhere
items.description: Explore secret leaks via env vars, logs, Git, images, and learn proper secret management patterns

items.title: Day 78 - Container Escape - Breaking Out of the Sandbox
items.description: Explore container escape vectors including privileged containers, hostPath mounts, capabilities, and host namespaces

items.title: Day 79 - Supply Chain Attacks - Poisoned Dependencies
items.description: Explore supply chain attack vectors including typosquatting, dependency confusion, image tampering, and detection strategies

items.title: Day 80 - Rate Limiting - API Throttling Gone Wrong
items.description: Explore API server rate limiting, Priority and Fairness queuing, client-side throttling, and troubleshooting throttled requests

items.title: Day 81 - Chaos Engineering - Breaking Things on Purpose
items.description: Explore chaos engineering with pod kills, network chaos, resource stress, dependency failures, and steady state validation

items.title: Day 82 - Configuration Drift - When Clusters Diverge
items.description: Explore configuration drift from manual changes, environment inconsistency, drift detection, and prevention strategies

items.title: Day 83 - Stateful Application Failures - When State Goes Wrong
items.description: Explore StatefulSet failure modes including volume provisioning errors, DNS breakage, pod ordering, update strategies, and split-brain scenarios

items.title: Day 84 - Ingress Controller Failures - Traffic Can't Route
items.description: Explore Ingress failure modes including missing controllers, wrong IngressClass, TLS misconfigurations, path type confusion, and backend endpoint issues

items.title: Day 90 - Multi-Container Pod Sidecar Failure Debugging
items.description: In this lab you will investigate a multi-container pod where a sidecar container is causing repeated restarts and resource contention. You will diagnose the root cause, fix the sidecar configuration, and verify that both containers run stably together. This scenario mirrors real-world service mesh and log forwarding failures.

items.title: Day 91 - Pod Pending Due to Node Selector Mismatch
items.description: In this lab you will diagnose a deployment where all pods are stuck in Pending state due to a node selector that does not match any available node labels. You will use scheduling events and node inspection to identify the mismatch, then choose between fixing the pod spec or labeling the appropriate node to resolve the issue. This scenario is extremely common during cluster upgrades and infrastructure migrations.

items.title: Day 92 - Service Selector Mismatch Traffic Debugging
items.description: In this lab you will investigate a service that is not routing traffic to its intended pods due to a label selector mismatch. You will inspect the service endpoints, compare selectors against pod labels, identify the discrepancy, and fix the configuration to restore traffic flow. This is one of the most common sources of silent production outages in Kubernetes.

items.title: Day 93 - Namespace Resource Quota Exhaustion Debugging
items.description: In this lab you will debug a namespace where a resource quota has been exhausted, preventing new pods from being scheduled even though the cluster nodes have available capacity. You will examine quota status, trace the failure through the deployment and replicaset event chain, and resolve the issue by either adjusting the quota or reducing resource requests. This is a critical skill for platform engineers managing shared Kubernetes clusters.

items.title: Day 94 - PVC Pending Due to StorageClass Misconfiguration
items.description: In this lab you will diagnose a PersistentVolumeClaim that is stuck in Pending state because of a StorageClass misconfiguration. You will trace the failure through PVC events, examine available StorageClass resources, identify the mismatch or missing provisioner, and fix the configuration to allow the volume to be provisioned and bound. This is a critical skill for anyone managing stateful workloads on Kubernetes.

items.title: Day 95 - ConfigMap Hot Reload and Stale Configuration Debugging
items.description: In this lab you will investigate the different behaviors of ConfigMap consumption methods and debug a situation where an application is reading stale configuration despite the ConfigMap having been updated. You will observe the difference between environment variable injection and volume mounting, understand the update propagation delay, and implement a reliable strategy for ensuring pods always use current configuration values. This scenario reflects real-world confusion that affects teams at every experience level.

items.title: Day 96 - Node Certificate Expiration Recovery Lab
items.description: In this lab you will diagnose a worker node that is showing as NotReady due to an expired kubelet client certificate. You will examine the symptoms at the node and pod level, understand how certificate expiration cascades through the cluster, identify the expired certificate, and work through the renewal process to restore node-to-API-server communication. This is a critical recovery skill for Kubernetes administrators responsible for long-running clusters.

items.title: Day 88 - KubeSkills Daily - Day 88 - HPA Edge Cases - When Autoscaling Makes Things Worse
Day 86 - KubeSkills Daily - Day 86 - CRD Validation Failures - When Custom Resources Won't Apply
Day 87 - KubeSkills Daily - Day 87 - DaemonSet Failures - When Node-Level Pods Won't Deploy
Day 89 - KubeSkills Daily - Day 89 - Node Affinity - Scheduling Where You Want
Day 85 - KubeSkills Daily - Day 85 - Job and CronJob Disasters - Batch Processing Failures
Day 97 - Node Affinity Scheduling Failures
items.description: A deployment has been created with node affinity requirements that no node in the cluster satisfies. All pods from this deployment are stuck in Pending state. Your job is to inspect the scheduler events, compare the affinity rules against actual node labels, and fix the configuration so pods can be scheduled correctly.

items.title: Day 98 - Pod Disruption Budget Rolling Update Stall
items.description: A deployment is performing a rolling update but has been stuck for an extended period with some pods on the old version and some on the new version. A Pod Disruption Budget is preventing any further progress. You will inspect the rollout, analyze the PDB configuration, and fix the constraints so the update can complete while still maintaining meaningful availability guarantees.

items.title: Day 99 - CNI Plugin Failure and Pod Network Breakdown
items.description: Pods across the cluster are in Running state but services are not responding to requests. Investigation reveals that pods on the same node can communicate but pods on different nodes cannot reach each other. The CNI plugin has a misconfiguration affecting cross-node routing. You will diagnose the networking failure, inspect CNI plugin state, and restore full pod-to-pod connectivity.

items.title: Day 100 - etcd Performance Degradation and Control Plane Slowdown
items.description: The cluster API server is responding slowly and controllers are reporting reconciliation errors. Metrics show that etcd request latency has spiked significantly. The etcd database has not been compacted or defragmented in a long time and has accumulated a large number of stale revisions. You will diagnose the etcd performance problem, perform maintenance operations, and restore normal cluster responsiveness.

items.title: Day 101 - Kubernetes Audit Log Investigation
items.description: A namespace containing production workloads has been deleted and the team needs to determine who performed the deletion and what other actions preceded it. The cluster has audit logging enabled and the logs have been preserved. You will learn to read and filter audit log records, identify the responsible identity, and understand the sequence of events that led to the incident.

items.title: Day 102 - Graceful Pod Shutdown and Connection Draining
items.description: A web service deployment causes connection errors on every rolling update as old pods terminate before load balancers stop routing traffic to them. The application does not implement graceful shutdown handling. You will configure preStop hooks and termination grace periods to eliminate connection drops during future deployments and verify the fix by running a rolling update while actively testing connectivity.

items.title: Day 103 - Kubernetes Version Skew Detection and Resolution
items.description: A cluster control plane has been upgraded but several worker nodes are still running an older version of the kubelet. Certain features that depend on newer API capabilities are failing on pods scheduled to the older nodes. You will audit all component versions, identify the nodes with problematic version skew, and upgrade those nodes to restore full cluster compatibility.

items.title: Day 104 - Node Affinity Scheduling Failures
items.description: A deployment is stuck with zero running pods because its required node affinity rule references a node label that does not exist in the cluster. You will use scheduler events and node inspection to identify the mismatch and apply the correct fix to get pods running.

items.title: Day 105 - Taints and Tolerations Scheduling Failures
items.description: A worker node has been tainted but no running workloads carry the matching toleration, causing that node to sit completely idle while other nodes are overloaded. You will identify the taint, add the correct toleration to the target deployment, and verify that pods begin scheduling on the previously empty node.

items.title: Day 106 - Pod Disruption Budget Blocking Node Drain
items.description: A node drain is indefinitely blocked because a pod disruption budget on a running deployment will not allow any pods to be evicted. You will identify which PDB is blocking the drain, understand why it cannot be satisfied, and apply a fix that allows the drain to complete while keeping the application available.

items.title: Day 107 - Priority Class Preemption Stealing Production Resources
items.description: A batch job has been given a higher priority class than the production deployment it shares a node with, and under CPU pressure the scheduler is preempting production pods to make room for batch work. You will identify the priority class misconfiguration, reassign priority classes appropriately, and verify that production pods are no longer vulnerable to preemption by lower-importance workloads.

items.title: Day 108 - ServiceAccount Token Mounting and Credential Exposure
items.description: Every pod in the target namespace has the default service account token mounted, including workloads that have no need to communicate with the Kubernetes API. You will audit the service accounts, identify which workloads should not have token access, and disable automatic token mounting on the appropriate resources.

items.title: Day 109 - Image Pull Secret Authentication Failures
items.description: A deployment is stuck in ImagePullBackOff because its image pull secret is either missing from the namespace or contains invalid credentials for the private registry. You will trace the authentication failure through pod events, inspect the secret, and apply a corrected secret that allows the image to be pulled successfully.

items.title: Day 110 - Cluster Autoscaler Scale-Up Failures
items.description: Pods are stuck Pending and the cluster autoscaler is deployed but is not provisioning new nodes. You will inspect the autoscaler logs, identify why scale-up is not being triggered, fix the pod resource requests and autoscaler configuration, and confirm that new nodes are provisioned and pods move to Running state.

items.title: Day 111 - Node Affinity Scheduling Failures
items.description: In this lab you will investigate why several pods are stuck in the pending state due to misconfigured node affinity rules. You will examine scheduler events, inspect node labels, and correct the affinity configuration to allow pods to schedule successfully. By the end you will understand the difference between required and preferred scheduling rules and how to debug affinity failures quickly.

items.title: Day 112 - Pod Disruption Budget Blocking Operations
items.description: In this lab you will encounter a scenario where a node drain is blocked by misconfigured Pod Disruption Budgets and a rolling deployment update has stalled. You will inspect the PDB configurations, understand why eviction is being rejected, and fix the PDB settings to allow normal cluster operations to resume. You will also observe how the rolling update controller responds once the PDB is corrected.

items.title: Day 113 - QoS Classes and Kubelet Eviction Ordering
items.description: In this lab you will observe how pods with different QoS classifications behave when node memory pressure is simulated. You will inspect the QoS class assigned to running pods, identify which pods are at highest risk of eviction, and update resource configurations to ensure critical workloads receive Guaranteed QoS status. You will also examine kubelet eviction events to understand the decision-making process.

items.title: Day 114 - Taints and Tolerations Scheduling Failures
items.description: In this lab you will diagnose why pods are not scheduling or are being evicted from nodes that have taints applied. You will examine node taints, understand the difference between taint effects, and add the correct tolerations to workload configurations so pods can run on the intended nodes. You will also observe what happens when a NoExecute taint is applied to a node that has running pods without a matching toleration.

items.title: Day 115 - ServiceAccount RBAC - Fixing API Permission Errors
items.description: In this lab you will investigate why an application pod is receiving 403 Forbidden errors when it tries to call the Kubernetes API. You will examine the pod's service account, check for existing role bindings, create the appropriate Role and RoleBinding resources, and verify that the application can successfully make API calls. You will also learn how to test API permissions directly from inside a pod.

items.title: Day 116 - Network Policy Debugging - Restoring Blocked Traffic
items.description: In this lab you will debug a three-tier application where network connectivity has broken after deny-all network policies were applied. You will examine the existing policies, trace which connections are being blocked, and add the correct allow rules to restore traffic flow between the web, API, and database layers. You will also ensure that DNS and health check traffic is not disrupted by the policies.

items.title: Day 117 - Cluster Autoscaler Node Addition With Pending Pods
items.description: In this lab you will investigate a scenario where the cluster autoscaler is functioning and adding nodes to the cluster but pods remain in the pending state. You will analyze the new nodes to understand their resource profile, examine the pending pods for scheduling constraints that prevent placement on the new nodes, and adjust either the pod specifications or the autoscaler node group configuration to resolve the mismatch. You will learn how to read autoscaler logs and understand scaling decisions.

items.title: Day 118 - Node Affinity Scheduling Surprises
items.description: In this lab you will debug a cluster where several deployments are failing to schedule or landing on incorrect nodes due to misconfigured node affinity rules. You will inspect node labels, review affinity expressions, identify the root causes, and correct each configuration so pods schedule as intended.

items.title: Day 119 - Pod Anti-Affinity Scaling Deadlock
items.description: In this lab you will debug a deployment that cannot scale to its desired replica count because of a required pod anti-affinity rule that exceeds the number of available nodes. You will analyze the scheduling constraints, understand the blocking condition, and reconfigure the anti-affinity policy to allow the deployment to scale correctly.

items.title: Day 120 - CPU Throttling - The Silent Performance Killer
items.description: In this lab you will investigate an application that is responding slowly despite appearing healthy in Kubernetes. You will use available metrics and resource inspection tools to discover that the container is being CPU throttled by its limit configuration, then adjust the resource limits and verify that performance improves.

items.title: Day 121 - OOMKilled - Memory Limit Disasters
items.description: In this lab you will investigate a deployment where containers are repeatedly being killed and restarted due to memory limit violations. You will examine the pod termination history, identify the memory usage pattern that triggers the OOMKill, and configure appropriate memory limits and requests to stabilize the workload.

items.title: Day 122 - Ephemeral Storage - The Silent Eviction
items.description: In this lab you will investigate a workload that keeps getting evicted from its node without any container crash or restart loop. You will trace the eviction back to ephemeral storage exhaustion by examining node and pod events, then reconfigure the workload to either increase the ephemeral storage limit or redirect disk usage to a proper volume.

items.title: Day 123 - PVC Binding - StatefulSet Startup Failures
items.description: In this lab you will diagnose a StatefulSet that cannot start because its persistent volume claims are stuck in Pending state. You will trace the binding failure to a StorageClass misconfiguration, understand how PVC binding relates to pod scheduling in a StatefulSet, and correct the storage configuration to allow all replicas to start successfully.

items.title: Day 124 - Control Plane Certificate Expiration - Cascading Failures
items.description: In this lab you will work through a cluster experiencing certificate expiration failures that are causing control plane communication errors. You will inspect the certificates used by each control plane component, identify which are expired or about to expire, and execute the renewal process to restore full cluster functionality.
